name: sqlmap
title: sqlmap

commands.name: sqlmap

arguments.description: Show basic help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            Show basic help message and exit

arguments.description: Show advanced help message and exit
arguments.raw: -hh                   Show advanced help message and exit

arguments.description: Show program's version number and exit
arguments.raw: --version             Show program's version number and exit

arguments.description: Verbosity level: 0-6 (default 1)
arguments.raw: -v VERBOSE            Verbosity level: 0-6 (default 1)

arguments.description: Target URL (e.g. "http://www.site.com/vuln.php?id=1")
arguments.raw: -u URL, --url=URL   Target URL (e.g. "http://www.site.com/vuln.php?id=1")

arguments.description: Process Google dork results as target URLs
arguments.raw: -g GOOGLEDORK       Process Google dork results as target URLs

arguments.description: Data string to be sent through POST (e.g. "id=1")
arguments.raw: --data=DATA         Data string to be sent through POST (e.g. "id=1")

arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value (e.g. "PHPSESSID=a8d127e..")
arguments.raw: --cookie=COOKIE     HTTP Cookie header value (e.g. "PHPSESSID=a8d127e..")

arguments.description: Use randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header value
arguments.raw: --random-agent      Use randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header value

arguments.description: Use a proxy to connect to the target URL
arguments.raw: --proxy=PROXY       Use a proxy to connect to the target URL

arguments.description: Use Tor anonymity network
arguments.raw: --tor               Use Tor anonymity network

arguments.description: Check to see if Tor is used properly
arguments.raw: --check-tor         Check to see if Tor is used properly

arguments.description: Testable parameter(s)
arguments.raw: -p TESTPARAMETER    Testable parameter(s)

arguments.description: Force back-end DBMS to provided value
arguments.raw: --dbms=DBMS         Force back-end DBMS to provided value

arguments.description: Level of tests to perform (1-5, default 1)
arguments.raw: --level=LEVEL       Level of tests to perform (1-5, default 1)

arguments.description: Risk of tests to perform (1-3, default 1)
arguments.raw: --risk=RISK         Risk of tests to perform (1-3, default 1)

arguments.description: SQL injection techniques to use (default "BEUSTQ")
arguments.raw: --technique=TECH..  SQL injection techniques to use (default "BEUSTQ")

arguments.description: Retrieve everything
arguments.raw: -a, --all           Retrieve everything

arguments.description: Retrieve DBMS banner
arguments.raw: -b, --banner        Retrieve DBMS banner

arguments.description: Retrieve DBMS current user
arguments.raw: --current-user      Retrieve DBMS current user

arguments.description: Retrieve DBMS current database
arguments.raw: --current-db        Retrieve DBMS current database

arguments.description: Enumerate DBMS users password hashes
arguments.raw: --passwords         Enumerate DBMS users password hashes

arguments.description: Enumerate DBMS databases
arguments.raw: --dbs               Enumerate DBMS databases

arguments.description: Enumerate DBMS database tables
arguments.raw: --tables            Enumerate DBMS database tables

arguments.description: Enumerate DBMS database table columns
arguments.raw: --columns           Enumerate DBMS database table columns

arguments.description: Enumerate DBMS schema
arguments.raw: --schema            Enumerate DBMS schema

arguments.description: Dump DBMS database table entries
arguments.raw: --dump              Dump DBMS database table entries

arguments.description: Dump all DBMS databases tables entries
arguments.raw: --dump-all          Dump all DBMS databases tables entries

arguments.description: DBMS database to enumerate
arguments.raw: -D DB               DBMS database to enumerate

arguments.description: DBMS database table(s) to enumerate
arguments.raw: -T TBL              DBMS database table(s) to enumerate

arguments.description: DBMS database table column(s) to enumerate
arguments.raw: -C COL              DBMS database table column(s) to enumerate

arguments.description: Prompt for an interactive operating system shell
arguments.raw: --os-shell          Prompt for an interactive operating system shell

arguments.description: Prompt for an OOB shell, Meterpreter or VNC
arguments.raw: --os-pwn            Prompt for an OOB shell, Meterpreter or VNC

arguments.description: Never ask for user input, use the default behavior
arguments.raw: --batch             Never ask for user input, use the default behavior

arguments.description: Flush session files for current target
arguments.raw: --flush-session     Flush session files for current target

arguments.description: Simple wizard interface for beginner users
arguments.raw: --wizard            Simple wizard interface for beginner users

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# sqlmap -h
       ___
      __H__
___ ___[,]_____ ___ ___  {1.10.6#stable}
 |_ -| . [.]     | .'| . |
 |___|_  [,]_|_|_|__,|  _|
     |_|V...       |_|   https://sqlmap.org
 
 Usage: python3 sqlmap [options]
 
 Options:
 -h, --help            Show basic help message and exit
 -hh                   Show advanced help message and exit
 --version             Show program's version number and exit
 -v VERBOSE            Verbosity level: 0-6 (default 1)
 
 Target:
   At least one of these options has to be provided to define the
   target(s)
 
   -u URL, --url=URL   Target URL (e.g. "http://www.site.com/vuln.php?id=1")
   -g GOOGLEDORK       Process Google dork results as target URLs
 
 Request:
   These options can be used to specify how to connect to the target URL
 
   --data=DATA         Data string to be sent through POST (e.g. "id=1")
   --cookie=COOKIE     HTTP Cookie header value (e.g. "PHPSESSID=a8d127e..")
   --random-agent      Use randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header value
   --proxy=PROXY       Use a proxy to connect to the target URL
   --tor               Use Tor anonymity network
   --check-tor         Check to see if Tor is used properly
 
 Injection:
   These options can be used to specify which parameters to test for,
   provide custom injection payloads and optional tampering scripts
 
   -p TESTPARAMETER    Testable parameter(s)
   --dbms=DBMS         Force back-end DBMS to provided value
 
 Detection:
   These options can be used to customize the detection phase
 
   --level=LEVEL       Level of tests to perform (1-5, default 1)
   --risk=RISK         Risk of tests to perform (1-3, default 1)
 
 Techniques:
   These options can be used to tweak testing of specific SQL injection
   techniques
 
   --technique=TECH..  SQL injection techniques to use (default "BEUSTQ")
 
 Enumeration:
   These options can be used to enumerate the back-end database
   management system information, structure and data contained in the
   tables
 
   -a, --all           Retrieve everything
   -b, --banner        Retrieve DBMS banner
   --current-user      Retrieve DBMS current user
   --current-db        Retrieve DBMS current database
   --passwords         Enumerate DBMS users password hashes
   --dbs               Enumerate DBMS databases
   --tables            Enumerate DBMS database tables
   --columns           Enumerate DBMS database table columns
   --schema            Enumerate DBMS schema
   --dump              Dump DBMS database table entries
   --dump-all          Dump all DBMS databases tables entries
   -D DB               DBMS database to enumerate
   -T TBL              DBMS database table(s) to enumerate
   -C COL              DBMS database table column(s) to enumerate
 
 Operating system access:
   These options can be used to access the back-end database management
   system underlying operating system
 
   --os-shell          Prompt for an interactive operating system shell
   --os-pwn            Prompt for an OOB shell, Meterpreter or VNC
 
 General:
   These options can be used to set some general working parameters
 
   --batch             Never ask for user input, use the default behavior
   --flush-session     Flush session files for current target
 
 Miscellaneous:
   These options do not fit into any other category
 
   --wizard            Simple wizard interface for beginner users
 
 [!] to see full list of options run with '-hh'

examples.command:
sqlmap -u "http://192.168.1.250/?p=1&forumaction=search" --dbs
        ___
       __H__
 ___ ___[)]_____ ___ ___  {1.2.11#stable}
|_ -| . ["]     | .'| . |
|___|_  ["]_|_|_|__,|  _|
      |_|V          |_|   http://sqlmap.org

[!] legal disclaimer: Usage of sqlmap for attacking targets without prior mutual consent is illegal. It is the end user's responsibility to obey all applicable local, state and federal laws. Developers assume no liability and are not responsible for any misuse or damage caused by this program

[13:37:00] [INFO] testing connection to the target URL

SYNOPSIS

     -h, --help
            Show basic help message and exit

     -hh    Show advanced help message and exit

            Show program's version number and exit

            Verbosity level: 0-6 (default 1)

            get(s)

            URL

            These options can be used to customize the detection phase

     --level=LEVEL
            Level of tests to perform (1-5, default 1)

     --risk=RISK
            Risk of tests to perform (1-3, default 1)

            Techniques:

            Enumeration:

            These options can be used to enumerate the back-end  database  man-
            agement system information, structure and data contained in the ta-
            bles

            Retrieve everything

     -b, --banner
            Retrieve DBMS banner

     --current-user
            Retrieve DBMS current user

     --current-db

     --passwords
            Enumerate DBMS users password hashes

     --dbs  Enumerate DBMS databases

     --tables
            Enumerate DBMS database tables

     --columns
            Enumerate DBMS database table columns

     --schema
            Enumerate DBMS schema

     --dump
            Dump DBMS database table entries

            Dump all DBMS databases tables entries

     -T TBL
            DBMS database table(s) to enumerate

     -C COL
            DBMS database table column(s) to enumerate

            Operating system access:

            ment system underlying operating system

sqlmap (1)           - automatic SQL injection tool
sqlmapapi (1)        - automatic SQL injection tool, api server

commands.name: sqlmapapi
commands.description: Automatic SQL injection tool, api server

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit

arguments.description: Run as a REST-JSON API server
arguments.raw: -s, --server          Run as a REST-JSON API server

arguments.description: Run as a REST-JSON API client
arguments.raw: -c, --client          Run as a REST-JSON API client

arguments.description: Host of the REST-JSON API server (default "127.0.0.1")
arguments.raw: -H HOST, --host=HOST  Host of the REST-JSON API server (default "127.0.0.1")

arguments.description: Port of the REST-JSON API server (default 8775)
arguments.raw: -p PORT, --port=PORT  Port of the REST-JSON API server (default 8775)

arguments.description: Server (bottle) adapter to use (default "wsgiref")
arguments.raw: --adapter=ADAPTER     Server (bottle) adapter to use (default "wsgiref")

arguments.description: Set IPC database filepath (optional)
arguments.raw: --database=DATABASE   Set IPC database filepath (optional)

arguments.description: Basic authentication username (optional)
arguments.raw: --username=USERNAME   Basic authentication username (optional)

arguments.description: Basic authentication password (optional)
arguments.raw: --password=PASSWORD   Basic authentication password (optional)

 Usage: sqlmapapi [options]
 
 Options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -s, --server          Run as a REST-JSON API server
 -c, --client          Run as a REST-JSON API client
 -H HOST, --host=HOST  Host of the REST-JSON API server (default "127.0.0.1")
 -p PORT, --port=PORT  Port of the REST-JSON API server (default 8775)
 --adapter=ADAPTER     Server (bottle) adapter to use (default "wsgiref")
 --database=DATABASE   Set IPC database filepath (optional)
 --username=USERNAME   Basic authentication username (optional)
 --password=PASSWORD   Basic authentication password (optional)

local_help.man:
SQLMAPAPI(1)                     User Commands                     SQLMAPAPI(1)

NAME
     sqlmapapi - automatic SQL injection tool, api server

SYNOPSIS
     sqlmapapi [options]

OPTIONS
     -h, --help
            show this help message and exit

     -s, --server
            Run as a REST-JSON API server

     -c, --client
            Run as a REST-JSON API client

     -H HOST, --host=HOST
            Host of the REST-JSON API server (default "127.0.0.1")

     -p PORT, --port=PORT
            Port of the the REST-JSON API server (default 8775)

     --adapter=ADAPTER
            Server (bottle) adapter to use (default "wsgiref")

     --username=USERNAME
            Basic authentication username (optional)

     --password=PASSWORD
            Basic authentication password (optional)

sqlmapapi v1.7.10                 October 2023                     SQLMAPAPI(1)
local_help.apropos: sqlmapapi (1)        - automatic SQL injection tool, api server

apt_metadata.description:
automatic SQL injection tool
sqlmap goal is to detect and take advantage of SQL injection
vulnerabilities in web applications. Once it detects one or more SQL
injections on the target host, the user can choose among a variety of
options to perform an extensive back-end database management system
fingerprint, retrieve DBMS session user and database, enumerate users,
password hashes, privileges, databases, dump entire or user's specific
DBMS tables/columns, run his own SQL statement, read specific files on
the file system and more.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
